title: Accusative in Action — Transitive Verbs
description: Practice accusative with common transitive verbs: vidět, mít, pít, jíst, číst

exercises.prompt: Choose the correct accusative form — animate vs inanimate masculine

data.explanation: Muž is masculine animate (a person), so it changes in the accusative: muž → muže. Contrast with masculine inanimate nouns which don't change.

exercises.prompt: Choose the correct accusative form — masculine inanimate does NOT change

data.explanation: Hrad is masculine inanimate (a thing, not alive), so it does NOT change in the accusative: hrad → hrad. Compare with muž → muže (animate) which does change.

exercises.prompt: Translate using the correct accusative form

data.grammar_note: Káva (feminine, -a) → kávu in accusative. Piju kávu = I drink coffee.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the accusative form of the noun

data.explanation: Chleba is masculine inanimate, so it does NOT change in the accusative: chleba → chleba. Jím chleba = I eat bread.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the accusative form — remember feminine -a → -u

data.explanation: Kniha (feminine, -a) → knihu in accusative. Čtu knihu = I read a book.

exercises.prompt: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence

data.explanation: Já mám nového psa. = I have a new dog. Pes (masculine animate) → psa in the accusative. This sentence expresses possession using the accusative case.
Co piješ? = What are you drinking? The answer needs the accusative: káva → kávu. Piju kávu = I drink coffee.

exercises.prompt: Answer the question — Koho vidíš? (Whom do you see?)

data.explanation: Koho vidíš? = Whom do you see? Use the accusative of a masculine animate noun. Student (masculine animate, hard consonant) → studenta. Vidím studenta = I see a student.

exercises.prompt: Translate the negative sentence — accusative stays the same

data.grammar_note: The negative prefix ne- attaches to the verb. The accusative noun does not change in negative sentences: Nemám kávu = I don't have coffee. Káva → kávu (accusative, feminine -a → -u).

exercises.prompt: Listen and write what you hear

data.note: Two negative sentences with accusative objects. Nevidím muže (muž → muže, animate) and Nemám kávu (káva → kávu, feminine).

exercises.prompt: Complete the dialogue — ordering in a café

data.scenario: In a café — ordering a drink
accepted_answers: Prosím si kávu.|Dám si kávu.|Chci kávu.
data.explanation: When ordering, use accusative forms: prosím si kávu (káva → kávu). The waiter asks 'Co si dáte?' (What will you have?) and the customer responds with the accusative object.

exercises.prompt: Practice pronouncing sentences with transitive verbs and accusative objects

data.target_text: Vidím muže. Vidím hrad. Piju kávu. Mám psa.
data.translation_en: I see a man. I see a castle. I drink coffee. I have a dog.

exercises.answer_key: Vidím muže. Vidím hrad. Piju kávu. Mám psa.
